data.title: Polyrizon Announces Filing of European Patent Application for Its Breakthrough Drug Delivery Platform
data.description: On April 27, 2026, in Raanana, Israel, Polyrizon Ltd. (Nasdaq: PLRZ) shared that they filed a new patent application with the Europe Patent Office for their Trap & Target (T&T) platform technology. This application specifically aims to safeguard the Company’s innovative intranasal drug delivery system, which enhances medication effectiveness by prolonging their presence and ensuring direct contact with mucosal tissues. It complements Polyrizon’s recent U.S. patent application that covers fundamental elements of their two primary platform technologies: Capture and Contain (C&C™), a nasal blocking technology, and Trap and Target (T&T™), developed for advanced nasal drug delivery.

data.title: CORRECTION - Satellogic Schedules First Quarter Fiscal Year 2026 Results Conference Call on Tuesday, May 12, 2026 at 8:00 a.m. Eastern Time
data.description: Satellogic has announced that they will be holding a conference call to discuss the financial results for the first quarter of fiscal year 2026. The call is scheduled for Tuesday, March 12, 2026, at 8:00 a.m. Eastern Time. Investors and analysts interested in participating can tune in at that time to hear about the company's performance.

data.title: Strategy Buys 3,273 Bitcoin For $255 Million: MSTR Targets $205
data.description: Strategy Inc. purchased 3,273 Bitcoin (BTC) for $255 million at an average price of $77,906 per coin by April 24. The acquisition was funded solely through the sale of common shares, a shift from previous purchases funded by preferred stock sales. The company now owns a total of 818,334 BTC, bought for $61.81 billion with an average cost of $75,537 per Bitcoin. Strategy bought Bitcoin every week in April, spending a total of over $6.4 billion. CEO Michael Saylor hinted at the acquisition strategy. Full details can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: These Analysts Increase Their Forecasts On Baker Hughes After Better-Than-Expected Q1 Results
data.description: Baker Hughes Co. (NASDAQ: BKR) revealed strong first-quarter performance in its recent report post-market hours on Thursday. The company exceeded expectations with adjusted earnings per share at 58 cents, surpassing the analyst prediction of 49 cents. The quarterly sales stood at $6.587 billion, outperforming the estimated $6.335 billion. Despite disruptions in the Middle East, CEO Lorenzo Simonelli praised the team's high-level execution leading to results exceeding the guidance. For the upcoming quarter, the management forecasts revenue of $6.5 billion and adjusted EBITDA of $1.13 billion, under the assumption that the conflict persists through June without worsening.

data.title: Leasys Launches the Renewed Clickar Platform Across Europe
data.description: Leasys has unveiled the revamped Clickar* platform in Rome, enhancing its digital marketplace for pre-owned vehicles and bolstering its remarketing operations in Europe. The upgraded platform boasts enhanced performance, scalability, and user-friendliness. With a redesigned interface improving navigation and search capabilities, users can efficiently browse and assess the assortment of vehicles available. Initially tailored for professional traders, Clickar provides a revamped experience for purchasing used vehicles.

data.title: Boomi To Unveil the Future Of Data Activation and AI-Driven Innovation at Boomi World 2026
data.description: A renowned event by Boomi in Conshohocken, Pennsylvania will showcase how organizations worldwide are utilizing data to drive initiatives ranging from artificial intelligence to business intelligence. This premier global event will focus on demonstrating the importance of data utilization in various aspects and will witness participation from numerous organizations and experts.

data.title: Digital Ascension Group Expands C-Suite to Deepen Its Lead in End-to-End Digital Asset Wealth Management
data.description: A leading wealth management firm in Dallas, Digital Ascension Group, has recently appointed three new senior executives to its leadership team. This move is aimed at bolstering the platform's expansion in various areas such as investment management, product strategy, operations, and compliance. The new executives are set to play a crucial role in supporting the firm's growth phase as it caters to established investors, family offices, and registered investment advisors. The decision to strengthen the leadership team comes at a time when both institutional and individual investors are increasingly looking for comprehensive, advisory-driven solutions in navigating the digital landscape.

data.title: Enterprises Achieve Governed HR Transformation with SAP
data.description: A recent report by ISG reveals that enterprises in Stamford, Connecticut are adopting the SAP SuccessFactors platform to revamp their HR processes. This initiative involves implementing scalable rollout models and effectively incorporating AI components. According to the report, this modernization effort is well-governed and aims to enhance HR operations for businesses. The transition to SAP SuccessFactors indicates a trend towards leveraging advanced technologies in managing human resources effectively.

data.title: Gloat Brings Agentic HR to Microsoft 365 Copilot and Microsoft Teams
data.description: In New York, Gloat has integrated Agentic HR features into Microsoft 365 Copilot and Microsoft Teams, enabling employees to access talent capabilities within their existing work tools. The company's AI agents provide workforce insights without users having to switch platforms. The Gloat Copilot Agent is now accessible through the Microsoft Agent Store, offering seamless integration for employees, managers, and HR teams.

data.title: MeridianLink Announces New Board Members to Accelerate Investment and Growth
data.description: In Irvine, California, MeridianLink, a digital lending company, recently added six industry experts to its Board of Directors and appointed David Murphy as the new Chairman.
